name: Skull and Bones Society
description: The Skull and Bones Society, officially Russell Trust Association, is a Yale University senior secret society founded in 1832. It's known for its secrecy, elite membership, and alleged influence in American politics and business. Conspiracy theories surrounding the society claim it's a powerful cabal controlling world events, pulling strings behind the scenes, and promoting its members to positions of power to further a hidden agenda. Proponents cite the disproportionate number of Skull and Bones members who have attained high-ranking positions in government, finance, and intelligence agencies as evidence of this manipulation. They point to the oaths of secrecy taken by members, the rituals performed within the society's 'Tomb,' and the society's alleged connections to influential families as further proof of its nefarious activities. Mainstream perspectives view the Skull and Bones Society as an elite social club with a history of privilege and networking. While acknowledging the success of many members, historians and sociologists attribute this to the advantages afforded by attending an Ivy League university and belonging to a prestigious social network, rather than a deliberate conspiracy. They emphasize that correlation does not equal causation and that the society's influence is often overstated. The secrecy surrounding the society fuels speculation, but concrete evidence of a coordinated conspiracy is lacking. Critics also point out that many other fraternities and secret societies exist with similar structures and rituals, yet do not attract the same level of scrutiny.
subtitle: Yale's Secret Society and Conspiracy

claims.claim: Skull and Bones controls world events.
claims.evidence: High-ranking members in government and business.
claims.criticism: Correlation does not equal causation; success due to privilege.

claims.claim: Members are sworn to a secret agenda.
claims.evidence: Oaths of secrecy and bizarre rituals.
claims.criticism: Secrecy is common in many societies; rituals are symbolic.

claims.claim: The Society promotes its members to power.
claims.criticism: Yale's network and elite status provide advantages.

keyFigures.name: Antony C. Sutton
keyFigures.role: Author who popularized conspiracy theories about Skull and Bones

keyFigures.name: Alexandra Robbins
keyFigures.role: Investigative journalist who wrote 'Secrets of the Tomb'

keyFigures.name: Ron Rosenbaum
keyFigures.role: Journalist who investigated and wrote about the society.

timeline.event: Skull and Bones Society founded at Yale University
Society's reputation for elitism begins to grow
Conspiracy theories about the society become widespread
Skull and Bones remains a subject of speculation and debate

sources.author: Antony C. Sutton

sources.title: Antony C. Sutton
